title: The Far Bend Memory

features.name: the far shore
features.desc-on-touch: The bank you haven't walked yet. It's identical to this one in all the ways that matter, but the water between you has learned a name the archive doesn't know.

features.name: crossing stones
features.desc-on-touch: Rocks set in the shallows, each one touched by hands that crossed before the archive began to remember. Your hand fits perfectly in the grooves.

features.name: the river's memory
features.desc-on-touch: Water flowing past where the bend deepens. It carries the names of all the places it has been, speaking them in a voice too wide for any single word.

features.name: shadow of the far bank
features.desc-on-touch: Your own silhouette on the water, but stretched toward the other side. It arrives before you do. It knows the far bank's name already.

features.name: weathered crossing post
features.desc-on-touch: Worn smooth by years of hands learning the river's way across. The post is older than the archive. The river chose to teach it that.

inscription: the bend continues downstream and the river teaches that crossing is always half remembering and half forgetting

file.name: CrossingRecords_FarBank_1962-1999.txt
file.note: Entries in two hands. One records who crossed. The other records what the crossing forgot. They drift further apart with each year.

reverie.title: REVERIE — crossing toward the far name
narr: She's wading ahead of you, the cold water up to
her thigh. She turns and speaks a word—not in any
language the archive carries—and the far bank
answers with the same shape of sound. You follow
the stones as she names each one. By the time you
reach the other side, you've forgotten which shore.

exits.label: back upriver to the wide water
downriver, where the bend has already forgotten what it taught
across the water to the far bank and its unnamed ground
deeper into the riverbed, to where old crossings sleep
